comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Implemented, commit f4d0c02c3: config-check.sh check 5 (yaml.safe_load extraction, compose-up.sh --print-volumes enumeration, loud enumeration-failure path) + two embedded self-tests (known-bad fixture detected; missing script fails loud). make config-check green on the stacked tree (AC2).

compiled_state.description:
CI drift gate: every `external: true` volume declared in `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` must be provisioned by `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh`, enforced in `make config-check` (already wired into test.yml AND gerrit-verify.yaml), so a compose-file edit that adds an external volume without a matching provisioning change can never reach `main`. Shift-left gate for incident 2731 (commit 10837ca88 added `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets: external: true` without updating compose-up.sh; every `compose up` then failed "external volume not found" for 41h).

## Dependencies / rollout
Gated on bug e991-e347-3444-427c (compose-up.sh provisioning fix) landing FIRST (declared `depends_on` edge): until that fix lands, this check correctly reports the live drift and would fail CI on main. Rollout = land as a stacked Gerrit change directly on top of e991's commit; both changes pass the Verified gate on the stacked tree, and submit order (bottom-first) guarantees main is never in a state where the gate fails.

## What (mechanism â€” single source of truth)
- `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh`: hoist the currently-duplicated hardcoded subdir list into ONE top-level variable (`SITE_SUBDIRS="git index cache db etc logs plugins reviewbot reviewbot-tickets"` â€” the 8 existing entries plus the missing one e991 adds), with volume-name derivation (`gerrit_` + subdir with `-` â†’ `_`) in one shared helper function. The two provisioning loops AND the new `--print-volumes` mode all iterate that same variable through that same helper â€” there is no second copy of the list or of the derivation to drift.
- `--print-volumes` mode: when `${1:-} == --print-volumes` (defaulted access â€” the script runs under `set -u`, so a bare `$1` would abort the normal zero-arg boot path), print the derived external volume names (one per line) and `exit 0`. The branch is placed immediately after the variable/helper definitions and BEFORE any side-effecting section (dnf/systemctl/docker/fetch-secrets), so it is side-effect-free by construction.
- `infra/scripts/config-check.sh` check 5: extract `external: true` volume names from docker-compose.yml with `python3` + `yaml.safe_load` (the same interpreter+library dependency check 3 already uses â€” no grep/awk heuristics over multi-line YAML). Run `compose-up.sh --print-volumes` capturing output AND exit code; a non-zero exit or empty output is itself a FAIL ("could not enumerate provisioned volumes") â€” never treated as an empty provisioned set. Fail naming each missing volume and pointing at compose-up.sh as the fix site.
- Self-test (pattern-regression guard) embedded in check 5: run the same extraction+comparison against an inline synthetic compose snippet declaring `gerrit_notprovisioned: external: true`; assert the check DETECTS it (non-zero, message contains the volume name and `compose-up.sh`). A silent false-negative regression of the extraction logic fails CI.
- The `--print-volumes` contract is documented in both script headers (compose-up.sh: "consumed by config-check.sh check 5"; config-check.sh: "requires compose-up.sh --print-volumes").

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC1 (red / self-test): the embedded synthetic fixture with `gerrit_notprovisioned` makes the drift check exit non-zero with a message naming `gerrit_notprovisioned` and containing `compose-up.sh` â€” proven automatically on every `make config-check` run.
- [ ] AC2 (green, in-session): `make config-check` exits 0 on this change's tree (stacked on e991, so `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets` is provisioned) â€” proven by running `make config-check` in the worktree before push and by the Verified vote on the stacked change.
- [ ] AC3 (no side effects, deterministic): running `bash infra/scripts/compose-up.sh --print-volumes` with `PATH` pointing at stub `docker`/`dnf`/`systemctl` executables that each write a canary file prints the expected volume list (including `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets`), exits 0, and leaves NO canary file â€” proving no side-effecting binary was invoked. Proving command recorded in verify_commands.
- [ ] AC4 (enumeration failure is loud, automated): check 5 embeds a second self-test that runs the same enumeration helper against a nonexistent script path and asserts it reports "could not enumerate provisioned volumes" (non-zero) rather than passing on an empty set â€” proven automatically on every `make config-check` run, same as AC1.


## Alternative considered (and why rejected)
Parsing compose-up.sh's provisioning loop textually from config-check.sh (no new mode) was rejected: a grep/awk heuristic over bash silently drifts out of sync with the script's format (the same failure class this gate exists to prevent). `--print-volumes` executes the real list through the real derivation helper, so the check can only report what the script would actually do. Single consumer is acceptable: the mode is 4 lines and sits beside the data it exposes.
- [ ] AC5 (contract documented): both script headers document the `--print-volumes` contract (compose-up.sh: "consumed by config-check.sh check 5"; config-check.sh: "requires compose-up.sh --print-volumes") â€” proven by grep in the change diff.

file_impact.reason: add check 5: external-volume drift gate + self-test

compiled_state.title: CI drift gate: every external volume in docker-compose.yml must be provisioned by compose-up.sh
